title: Ruddia fordiana
intro: Ruddia fordiana is a plant species that belongs to the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as a medicinal shrub or herb. The health benefits of Ruddia fordiana include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Ruddia fordiana is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Ruddia fordiana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. It belongs to the tribe Spermacoceae. The genus Ruddia is a small group of flowering plants. Ruddia fordiana is native to Sri Lanka. The classification of Ruddia fordiana is also recognized in various botanical databases and resources.
